speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: What is this forest called?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: ’Tis Gaultree Forest, an ’t shall please your Grace.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Here stand, my lords, and send discoverers forth

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To know the numbers of our enemies.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: We have sent forth already.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: ’Tis well done.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: My friends and brethren in these great affairs,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I must acquaint you that I have received

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: New-dated letters from Northumberland,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Their cold intent, tenor, and substance, thus:

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Here doth he wish his person, with such powers

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: As might hold sortance with his quality,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The which he could not levy; whereupon

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: He is retired, to ripe his growing fortunes,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To Scotland, and concludes in hearty prayers

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That your attempts may overlive the hazard

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And fearful meeting of their opposite.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Thus do the hopes we have in him touch ground

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And dash themselves to pieces.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Now, what news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: West of this forest, scarcely off a mile,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: In goodly form comes on the enemy,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, by the ground they hide, I judge their number

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Upon or near the rate of thirty thousand.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The just proportion that we gave them out.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Let us sway on and face them in the field.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: What well-appointed leader fronts us here?

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: I think it is my Lord of Westmoreland.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Health and fair greeting from our general,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The Prince Lord John and Duke of Lancaster.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Say on, my Lord of Westmoreland, in peace,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: What doth concern your coming.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Then, my lord,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Unto your Grace do I in chief address

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The substance of my speech. If that rebellion

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Came like itself, in base and abject routs,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Led on by bloody youth, guarded with rage,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And countenanced by boys and beggary—

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I say, if damned commotion so appeared

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: In his true, native, and most proper shape,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: You, reverend father, and these noble lords

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Had not been here to dress the ugly form

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Of base and bloody insurrection

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: With your fair honors. You, Lord Archbishop,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Whose see is by a civil peace maintained,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Whose beard the silver hand of peace hath touched,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Whose learning and good letters peace hath tutored,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Whose white investments figure innocence,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The dove and very blessèd spirit of peace,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Wherefore do you so ill translate yourself

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Out of the speech of peace, that bears such grace,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Into the harsh and boist’rous tongue of war,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Turning your books to graves, your ink to blood,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Your pens to lances, and your tongue divine

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To a loud trumpet and a point of war?

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Wherefore do I this? So the question stands.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Briefly, to this end: we are all diseased

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And with our surfeiting and wanton hours

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Have brought ourselves into a burning fever,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And we must bleed for it; of which disease

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Our late King Richard, being infected, died.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: But, my most noble Lord of Westmoreland,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I take not on me here as a physician,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Nor do I as an enemy to peace

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Troop in the throngs of military men,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: But rather show awhile like fearful war

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To diet rank minds sick of happiness

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And purge th’ obstructions which begin to stop

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Our very veins of life. Hear me more plainly.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I have in equal balance justly weighed

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: What wrongs our arms may do, what wrongs we suffer,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And find our griefs heavier than our offenses.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: We see which way the stream of time doth run

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And are enforced from our most quiet there

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: By the rough torrent of occasion,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And have the summary of all our griefs,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: When time shall serve, to show in articles;

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Which long ere this we offered to the King

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And might by no suit gain our audience.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: When we are wronged and would unfold our griefs,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: We are denied access unto his person

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Even by those men that most have done us wrong.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The dangers of the days but newly gone,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Whose memory is written on the earth

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: With yet-appearing blood, and the examples

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Of every minute’s instance, present now,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Hath put us in these ill-beseeming arms,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Not to break peace or any branch of it,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: But to establish here a peace indeed,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Concurring both in name and quality.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Whenever yet was your appeal denied?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Wherein have you been gallèd by the King?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: What peer hath been suborned to grate on you,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That you should seal this lawless bloody book

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Of forged rebellion with a seal divine

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And consecrate commotion’s bitter edge?

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: My brother general, the commonwealth,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To brother born an household cruelty,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I make my quarrel in particular.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: There is no need of any such redress,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Or if there were, it not belongs to you.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Why not to him in part, and to us all

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That feel the bruises of the days before

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And suffer the condition of these times

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To lay a heavy and unequal hand

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Upon our honors?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: O, my good Lord Mowbray,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Construe the times to their necessities,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And you shall say indeed it is the time,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And not the King, that doth you injuries.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Yet for your part, it not appears to me

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Either from the King or in the present time

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That you should have an inch of any ground

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To build a grief on. Were you not restored

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To all the Duke of Norfolk’s seigniories,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Your noble and right well remembered father’s?

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: What thing, in honor, had my father lost

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That need to be revived and breathed in me?

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: The King that loved him, as the state stood then,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Was force perforce compelled to banish him,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And then that Henry Bolingbroke and he,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Being mounted and both rousèd in their seats,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Their neighing coursers daring of the spur,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Their armèd staves in charge, their beavers down,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Their eyes of fire sparkling through sights of steel,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And the loud trumpet blowing them together,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Then, then, when there was nothing could have stayed

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: My father from the breast of Bolingbroke,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: O, when the King did throw his warder down—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: His own life hung upon the staff he threw—

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Then threw he down himself and all their lives

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That by indictment and by dint of sword

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Have since miscarried under Bolingbroke.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: You speak, Lord Mowbray, now you know not what.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The Earl of Hereford was reputed then

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: In England the most valiant gentleman.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Who knows on whom fortune would then have smiled?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: But if your father had been victor there,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: He ne’er had borne it out of Coventry;

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: For all the country in a general voice

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Cried hate upon him; and all their prayers and love

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Were set on Hereford, whom they doted on

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And blessed and graced, indeed more than the King.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: But this is mere digression from my purpose.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Here come I from our princely general

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To know your griefs, to tell you from his Grace

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That he will give you audience; and wherein

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: It shall appear that your demands are just,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: You shall enjoy them, everything set off

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That might so much as think you enemies.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: But he hath forced us to compel this offer,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And it proceeds from policy, not love.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Mowbray, you overween to take it so.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: This offer comes from mercy, not from fear.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: For, lo, within a ken our army lies,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Upon mine honor, all too confident

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To give admittance to a thought of fear.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Our battle is more full of names than yours,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Our men more perfect in the use of arms,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Our armor all as strong, our cause the best.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Then reason will our hearts should be as good.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Say you not then our offer is compelled.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Well, by my will, we shall admit no parley.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That argues but the shame of your offense.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: A rotten case abides no handling.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Hath the Prince John a full commission,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: In very ample virtue of his father,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To hear and absolutely to determine

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Of what conditions we shall stand upon?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: That is intended in the General’s name.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I muse you make so slight a question.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Then take, my Lord of Westmoreland, this schedule,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: For this contains our general grievances.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Each several article herein redressed,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: All members of our cause, both here and hence

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That are insinewed to this action,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Acquitted by a true substantial form

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And present execution of our wills

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To us and to our purposes confined,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: We come within our awful banks again

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And knit our powers to the arm of peace.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: This will I show the General. Please you, lords,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: In sight of both our battles we may meet,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And either end in peace, which God so frame,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Or to the place of difference call the swords

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Which must decide it.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: My lord, we will do so.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: There is a thing within my bosom tells me

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That no conditions of our peace can stand.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Fear you not that. If we can make our peace

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Upon such large terms and so absolute

speaker: HASTINGS
text: As our conditions shall consist upon,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Our peace shall stand as firm as rocky mountains.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Yea, but our valuation shall be such

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That every slight and false-derivèd cause,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Yea, every idle, nice, and wanton reason,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Shall to the King taste of this action,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That, were our royal faiths martyrs in love,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: We shall be winnowed with so rough a wind

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: That even our corn shall seem as light as chaff,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: And good from bad find no partition.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: No, no, my lord. Note this: the King is weary

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Of dainty and such picking grievances,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: For he hath found to end one doubt by death

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Revives two greater in the heirs of life;

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And therefore will he wipe his tables clean

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And keep no telltale to his memory

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That may repeat and history his loss

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To new remembrance. For full well he knows

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: He cannot so precisely weed this land

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: As his misdoubts present occasion;

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: His foes are so enrooted with his friends

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That, plucking to unfix an enemy,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: He doth unfasten so and shake a friend;

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: So that this land, like an offensive wife

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That hath enraged him on to offer strokes,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: As he is striking holds his infant up

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And hangs resolved correction in the arm

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: That was upreared to execution.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Besides, the King hath wasted all his rods

speaker: HASTINGS
text: On late offenders, that he now doth lack

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The very instruments of chastisement,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: So that his power, like to a fangless lion,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: May offer but not hold.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: ’Tis very true,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And therefore be assured, my good Lord Marshal,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: If we do now make our atonement well,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Our peace will, like a broken limb united,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Grow stronger for the breaking.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Be it so.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Here is returned my Lord of Westmoreland.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The Prince is here at hand. Pleaseth your Lordship

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: To meet his Grace just distance ’tween our armies.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Your Grace of York, in God’s name then set forward.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Before, and greet his Grace.—My lord, we come.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: You are well encountered here, my cousin Mowbray.—

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Good day to you, gentle Lord Archbishop,—

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And so to you, Lord Hastings, and to all.—

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: My Lord of York, it better showed with you

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: When that your flock, assembled by the bell,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Encircled you to hear with reverence

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Your exposition on the holy text

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Than now to see you here, an iron man talking,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Cheering a rout of rebels with your drum,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Turning the word to sword, and life to death.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: That man that sits within a monarch’s heart

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And ripens in the sunshine of his favor,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Would he abuse the countenance of the King,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Alack, what mischiefs might he set abroach

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: In shadow of such greatness! With you, Lord Bishop,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: It is even so. Who hath not heard it spoken

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: How deep you were within the books of God,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: To us the speaker in His parliament,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: To us th’ imagined voice of God Himself,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: The very opener and intelligencer

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Between the grace, the sanctities, of heaven,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And our dull workings? O, who shall believe

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: But you misuse the reverence of your place,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Employ the countenance and grace of heaven

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: As a false favorite doth his prince’s name,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: In deeds dishonorable? You have ta’en up,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Under the counterfeited zeal of God,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: The subjects of His substitute, my father,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And both against the peace of heaven and him

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Have here up-swarmed them.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Good my Lord of Lancaster,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I am not here against your father’s peace,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: But, as I told my Lord of Westmoreland,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The time misordered doth, in common sense,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Crowd us and crush us to this monstrous form

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To hold our safety up. I sent your Grace

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The parcels and particulars of our grief,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: The which hath been with scorn shoved from the court,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Whereon this Hydra son of war is born,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Whose dangerous eyes may well be charmed asleep

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: With grant of our most just and right desires,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And true obedience, of this madness cured,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Stoop tamely to the foot of majesty.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: If not, we ready are to try our fortunes

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: To the last man.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And though we here fall down,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: We have supplies to second our attempt;

speaker: HASTINGS
text: If they miscarry, theirs shall second them,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And so success of mischief shall be born,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And heir from heir shall hold his quarrel up

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Whiles England shall have generation.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: You are too shallow, Hastings, much too shallow

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: To sound the bottom of the after-times.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Pleaseth your Grace to answer them directly

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: How far forth you do like their articles.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I like them all, and do allow them well,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And swear here by the honor of my blood

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: My father’s purposes have been mistook,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And some about him have too lavishly

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Wrested his meaning and authority.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Archbishop
                .
                 
              My lord, these griefs shall be with speed redressed;

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Upon my soul, they shall. If this may please you,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Discharge your powers unto their several counties,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: As we will ours, and here, between the armies,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Let’s drink together friendly and embrace,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: That all their eyes may bear those tokens home

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Of our restorèd love and amity.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I take your princely word for these redresses.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I give it you, and will maintain my word,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And thereupon I drink unto your Grace.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Go, captain, and deliver to the army

speaker: HASTINGS
text: This news of peace. Let them have pay, and part.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I know it will well please them. Hie thee, captain.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: To you, my noble Lord of Westmoreland.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I pledge your Grace, and if you knew what pains

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I have bestowed to breed this present peace,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: You would drink freely. But my love to you

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Shall show itself more openly hereafter.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: I do not doubt you.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I am glad of it.—

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Health to my lord and gentle cousin, Mowbray.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: You wish me health in very happy season,

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: For I am on the sudden something ill.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Against ill chances men are ever merry,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: But heaviness foreruns the good event.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Therefore be merry, coz, since sudden sorrow

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Serves to say thus: “Some good thing comes tomorrow.”

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Believe me, I am passing light in spirit.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: So much the worse if your own rule be true.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: The word of peace is rendered. Hark how they shout.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: This had been cheerful after victory.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: A peace is of the nature of a conquest,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: For then both parties nobly are subdued,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And neither party loser.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Go, my lord,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: And let our army be dischargèd too.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Archbishop
                .
                 
              And, good my lord, so please you, let our trains

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: March by us, that we may peruse the men

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: We should have coped withal.

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Go, good Lord Hastings,

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: And ere they be dismissed, let them march by.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I trust, lords, we shall lie tonight together.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Now, cousin, wherefore stands our army still?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: The leaders, having charge from you to stand,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Will not go off until they hear you speak.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: They know their duties.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: My lord, our army is dispersed already.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Like youthful steers unyoked, they take their courses

speaker: HASTINGS
text: East, west, north, south, or, like a school broke up,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Each hurries toward his home and sporting-place.

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Good tidings, my Lord Hastings, for the which

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: I do arrest thee, traitor, of high treason.—

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: And you, Lord Archbishop, and you, Lord Mowbray,

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Of capital treason I attach you both.

speaker: MOWBRAY
text: Is this proceeding just and honorable?

speaker: WESTMORELAND
text: Is your assembly so?

speaker: ARCHBISHOP
text: Will you thus break your faith?

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I pawned thee none.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I promised you redress of these same grievances

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Whereof you did complain, which, by mine honor,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I will perform with a most Christian care.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: But for you rebels, look to taste the due

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Meet for rebellion and such acts as yours.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Most shallowly did you these arms commence,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Fondly brought here, and foolishly sent hence.—

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Strike up our drums; pursue the scattered stray.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: God, and not we, hath safely fought today.—

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Some guard these traitors to the block of death,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Treason’s true bed and yielder-up of breath.
